Appearance
From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

2025 Africa Cup of Nations squads
← 2023
2027 →
International football competition

The 2025 Africa Cup of Nations was an international football tournament held in Morocco from 21 December 2025 to 18 January 2026. The 24 participating national teams were required to register a squad with a maximum 28 players.[1]

The age listed for each player is on 21 December 2025, the first day of the tournament. The numbers of caps and goals listed for each player do not include any matches played after the start of the tournament. The club listed is the club for which the player last played a competitive match before the tournament.[a] The nationality for each club reflects the national association (not the league) to which the club is affiliated. A flag is included for coaches who are of a different nationality than their own national team.

[edit]

Burkina Faso's 25 player squad was announced on 8 December.[21] Konaté, Tiendrébéogo and Yoda were included in the official squad list as reserve players.[22]

Notes

[edit]

  2. ^ a b Monaco is a Monégasque club playing in the French football league system.

  6. ^ "Angola name final 28-man squad for TotalEnergies CAF AFCON 2025 as Beaumelle targets historic breakthrough". Confederation of African Football. 3 December 2025. Retrieved 12 December 2025.
  7. ^ "Egypt name final 28-man squad for TotalEnergies CAF AFCON 2025, Salah leads Pharaohs' charge". Confederation of African Football. 2 December 2025. Retrieved 12 December 2025.

  17. ^ "DR Congo name 26-man squad for TotalEnergies CAF Africa Cup of Nations, Morocco 2025". Confederation of African Football. 11 December 2025. Retrieved 12 December 2025.
  18. ^ "Senegal take fitness gamble as Thiaw names injured trio in AFCON squad". CAFOnline.com. 14 December 2025. Retrieved 14 December 2025.
  19. ^ "Senegal Confirm Ousseynou Niang as Replacement for Assane Diao at 2025 AFCON". dailysports.net. 22 December 2025. Retrieved 22 December 2025.
  20. ^ "Blow for Algeria as key midfielder ruled out of Cup of Nations". Reuters. 19 December 2025. Retrieved 24 December 2025.{{cite web}}: CS1 maint: url-status (link)
  21. ^ "Burkina Faso name final 25-man squad for TotalEnergies AFCON 2025, emerging talent called". Confederation of African Football. 11 December 2025. Retrieved 12 December 2025.
  22. ^ "CAN Morocco 2025: 25 Stallions selected to represent Burkina Faso". OneFootball. 8 December 2025. Retrieved 22 December 2025.
  23. ^ "Aubameyang leads Gabon's bold 28-man squad as Mouyouma blends experience, comebacks and youth". Confederation of African Football. 11 December 2025. Retrieved 12 December 2025.
  24. ^ "Title holders Cote d'Ivoire name 26-man TotalEnergies CAF AFCON squad as Zaha returns and Adingra misses out". Confederation of African Football. 9 December 2025. Retrieved 12 December 2025.
  25. ^ "Conde unveils Mozambique 25-man final squad as Mambas set sights on TotalEnergies CAF AFCON Morocco 2025". Confederation of African Football. 8 December 2025. Retrieved 12 December 2025.

Notes
The 2021 and 2023 tournaments were actually held in 2022 and 2024 respectively.
There were no 1957 and 1959 qualifications as places were given by invitation only.
The "finals" articles for 1959 and 1976 are about the decisive matches of final group stages.
